body_txt: So it turns out we're making an article about the First-Person Smartass, and now I have to tell you everything about the type of narrator who speaks in first-person (because you obviously didn't get that from the name) and describes events in the tone of a Deadpan Snarker . He does this since he knows that, contrary to the popular misconception, narration isn't about, y’know, letting the reader in on the plot; it's about sharing with them every remotely entertaining stray thought you have. This guy sometimes shows up in the Private Eye Monologue sort of work, but Urban Fantasy is where they're really thick on the ground. If you want to find one, just cast a fireball in some otherwise normal city and before you're done, a dozen wannabe-protagonists will jump at you from behind a corner and start throwing pithy remarks about how you're being cliché and violating the laws of thermodynamics or some shit like that. This guy also gets a lotta work in any book aimed at teens that doesn't drown itself in angst — because apparently every teen in the world either suffers like Hamlet or quips like Mercutio. Well, fine , that's... hyperbole, I suppose... but you have to admit the guy is an awfully convenient proxy for a clever author who wants to show the world how clever they are. Not to mention he can also function as an Audience Surrogate , incorporating and defusing a reader's skepticism with endless Lampshade Hanging of whatever bits of the story don't make sense. You can almost feel the enormous weight of the story's Willing Suspension of Disbelief crushing this poor bastard’s shoulders. And speaking of Shakespeare references, you can expect this guy to be intellectual and well-acquainted with pop culture (or at least the part of pop culture with which the author is familiar ), so he can make all the right clever references at the right time. Naturally, this won't prevent him from being described as uneducated, bad at school, or just plain Book Dumb ; these traits are apparently all the fucking rage for Audience Surrogates nowadays as people can't identify with someone who might possibly be a better person than they are . Unfortunately the character may end up being too smart, because the writer is afraid of looking stupid. And with Fan Fiction , a character of only moderate intelligence may be given uncharacteristically intelligent thoughts, and leave the readers with a sense of OOC-ness . (Not that fanfic writers were ever really good at maintaining characters, though.) Anyways, the Trope Namer was a review of Steven Brust's Dragaera series by The Library of Babel . And of course you're going to click that, because the "click hither and educate thyself" tone of that sentence just screams "fun." Compare Lemony Narrator . Or don’t, I don’t care how lazy you are. ... I don’t like my job.
